title: Shareholders Kick Against Postponment Of Presco Plc AGM … Seeks Regulators Intervention - FinancialEDGE
description: Following the planned postponement of Presco Plc's Annual General Meeting (AGM) due to a court order, some shareholders of the company have expressed their opposition, calling on the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) and the Nigerian Exchange Limited (NGX) to intervene and protect shareholders' investments. html:
Following the planned postponement of Presco Plc’s Annual General Meeting (AGM) due to a court order, some shareholders of the company have expressed their opposition, calling on the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) and the Nigerian Exchange Limited (NGX) to intervene and protect shareholders’ investments.

Mr. Adeleke Oladimeji, representing the Dedicated Shareholders Association, stated, “Some individuals are attempting to hold the company hostage and disrupt its processes by stopping the meeting.” He questioned, “How can shareholders who own just 14 units of shares afford to go to court and obtain a court injunction to stop a meeting when the company has not violated any laws?”

Mr. Tunde Badmus, representing the Pacesetters Shareholders Association, said, “The company has declared dividends, and shareholder approval is required for the dividend to be paid. Moreover, the company has incurred significant expenses in printing the annual accounts and renting a hall for the meeting.”

Mrs. Efunyemi Obideyi, representing the Ark of God Shareholders Association, expressed concern that shareholders might protest if the meeting is canceled, emphasizing that shareholders need to receive their dividends and that the audit committee elections must take place as scheduled.

All three associations are urging the SEC and NGX to intervene and ensure that the AGM holds as scheduled, to protect the interests of all shareholders.
